features: automation run dashboard with live posting status
inventory feed browser with DMS sync state
Facebook posting queue with retry status
automation health monitor with selector failure log

challenges.title: Facebook DOM Fragility — Selectors Break Without Warning
challenges.outcome: Could reduce selector-break downtime from days to minutes by decoupling selector definitions from automation logic and adding fallback resolution chains

challenges.title: Photo Upload Failures and CAPTCHA Interruptions
challenges.outcome: Could increase successful post completion rate from ~60% to 90%+ by adding resilient waits, retry backoff, and CAPTCHA detection with human-in-the-loop escalation

challenges.title: Listing Removal Reliability — Sold Vehicles Staying Live
challenges.outcome: Could eliminate manual removal work by hardening the delisting flow to handle FB session state, stale cookies, and re-auth triggers reliably

challenges.title: Motorcycle vs. Powersport Category Form Branching
challenges.outcome: Could handle both form schemas in a single automation run by detecting category at runtime and routing to the correct field-filling sequence automatically

coverLetterHooks: Facebook changes DOM constantly, selectors break
CAPTCHA prompts, slow loading, photo upload failures
listing removal stubbed out but barely tested
Bonus points if you've automated anything on Facebook before — you know how fun that is
screeningQuestion: Drop me a message with any relevant Playwright or browser automation work you've done. Bonus points if you've automated anything on Facebook before — you know how fun that is.
screeningAnswer: Built a real-time eBay monitor using webhook-based polling and a Playwright-backed scraper — the kind of work where the target site changes without notice and your automation has to stay resilient. Facebook Marketplace is a different level of fun: I've dealt with the DOM shuffle, rate limiting, and session state expiry. Happy to share specifics on a call or in a quick doc.

aestheticProfile.formatRationale: The job describes an existing desktop admin tool with a sidebar, dashboard, inventory table, and settings screen. The client already shared screenshots showing a dark-themed sidebar app with orange accent branding. A dashboard-app format mirrors the architecture they're already building and signals immediate comprehension of the product.
aestheticProfile.mood: high-stakes operational, technically precise, built for power users
aestheticProfile.colorDirection: electric orange on near-black at oklch(0.68 0.20 42) — matches the existing Full Throttle brand signaled in the screenshot description, high chroma for punch on dark backgrounds
aestheticProfile.justification: Client wrote technically and directly — 'TypeScript @ts-ignore', 'persistent browser profiles', 'headless and headed modes'. The existing app is dark-themed with orange accents (from screenshot context: 'dark theme, orange accents, FULL THROTTLE branding'). This is a desktop ops tool used by dealership power users who run automation jobs. Dark Premium is the natural fit: it signals technical credibility, matches the existing brand aesthetic, and reads as a professional automation platform rather than a generic SaaS dashboard.

designSignals: This client is a technical founder or developer who built the app themselves — they know exactly what Playwright selectors are, what a persistent browser profile means, and why CAPTCHA handling is hard. They won't be impressed by visual flair; they'll be impressed by a demo that shows the operational monitoring surfaces they actually need: a live posting queue with retry status, a failure log with selector context, a sync health indicator. The reference point for 'premium' to this client is something like Tauri's own dashboard or a DevOps monitoring tool — functional, dense, dark, and clearly built by someone who has written automation code before.
domainResearcherFocus: Focus on powersport dealership terminology: DMS (dealer management system — common vendors: CDK Global, Reynolds & Reynolds, Dealertrack, DealerSocket), inventory feed formats (CSV/XML with fields like VIN, make, model, year, trim, mileage, price, condition, photos array). Realistic vehicle entities: 2023 Honda CRF450R, 2024 Polaris RZR Pro R, 2022 Can-Am Spyder RT, 2023 Kawasaki Z900, 2024 Yamaha YXZ1000R. KPI ranges: a small dealership posts 20-80 vehicles/month, posting success rate 60-90%, avg listing age 30-90 days. Edge cases: vehicle sold same day as posting attempt, DMS feed includes duplicate VINs, photo array is empty or has broken URLs, FB session expires mid-run. Facebook Marketplace automation context: Playwright with chromium, persistent context at ~/.ft-browser-profile, form fields target category 'Motorcycles & Powersports' or 'Powersports' subcategory. Real tools in this space: Tauri, Svelte, Prisma + PostgreSQL, Zod schemas, BullMQ or similar for job queues.
